results.title: Unstable elements
results.creator_credit: Paul Morrison, Andy Metcalf, Gillian Slovo and Newsreel Collective
results.brief_description: Using revealing interviews with prominent scientists, and extraordinary newsreels from the 1940s and 1950s, “Unstable Elements” traces the history of nuclear Britain. Combining several stories in one film, this film also examines - through its fictional part - the way the tunnel vision of the atomic scientists is reflected in family life. A film for a wide audience.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Paul Morrison, Andy Metcalf, Gillian Slovo and Newsreel Collective

results.title: Unseen horizons
results.creator_credit: Bernard Till, Terry Ashwood and Associated British-Pathe
results.brief_description: A complete primary, secondary and specialised vocational educational system for the blind is maintained in Great Britain by the Royal National Institute for the Blind. All types of schools provided are shown.

results.title: Unseen harvest
results.creator_credit: Jim Northcott, Africa Film Service and British Information Services
results.brief_description: The Salamawe training school for the blind in Uganda. Blind students are taught to become farmers, and instruction from sighted Africans is given in many branches of land cultivation and in the handling of farm equipment. Rope and basket making, athletics and music, are enjoyed by the students.

results.title: Unseen enemies
results.creator_credit: Michael Clarke, Stuart Legg and Shell Film Unit
results.brief_description: Shows the ravages of such diseases as leprosy, malaria, elephantiasis, yaws, yellow fever in various countries and what is being done, mainly through the World Health Organisation to eradicate them.

results.title: University of flying
results.creator_credit: Maxwell Munden and Central Office of Information
results.brief_description: Men of all nationalities attend the Empire Test Pilot School at Cranfield, UK, for it is the only school of its kind in the world. Its purpose is to make men who are already experienced pilots into test pilots, for aircraft companies and Government establishments are always in need of test pilots.

results.title: Universities of Australia
results.creator_credit: Malcolm Otton, Geoffrey Bell and Australian National Film Board
results.brief_description: An account of the growth of Australia’s universities and a survey of their contribution to our national life. The technological contribution to the nation, based on the scientific work of the universities, is stressed and the need for additional and continued support for the institutions is emphasised.
